item.description:
Release date: 2026-02-10

New

  • **Metadata results are now more consistent across AI providers.** Filename, tags, and description generation now behaves more predictably when you switch between supported providers.
  • **The update banner now includes a full "What's New" view.** You can expand details in-app, review highlights, and choose to install now or be reminded later.

Improvements

  • **Metadata handling is more reliable when AI responses are incomplete.** NameQuick now recovers more often instead of failing, and continues with filename-only output when needed.
  • **Template placeholders are more reliable across languages and regions.** Date placeholders are more stable, and field matching now handles accented characters more accurately.
  • **Network error handling is faster and more resilient.** Temporary connection failures are detected earlier so stuck AI runs recover faster.
  • **PDF processing now defaults to fewer pages.** The default limit moved from 15 pages to 5 to keep processing faster and reduce unnecessary usage.

Bug Fixes

  • **Fixed credit balances resetting unexpectedly after sync.** Your balance now stays stable during normal account refreshes.
  • **Fixed several edge cases where metadata fallback could behave inconsistently.** Recovery behavior is now more predictable when a provider response fails mid-flow.
  • **Fixed provider-specific metadata request issues for OpenRouter and Gemini.** Metadata generation now succeeds more reliably in those paths.
  • **Fixed free-form naming output that could alter intended punctuation.** Meaningful hyphens are now preserved.
  • **Fixed BYOK trial and account identity continuity issues.** Access state and account identity now remain consistent across sessions and account transitions.
  • **Fixed update banner state and release-notes parsing issues.** Update details now display more reliably in different feed formats.

Release date: 2026-02-05

New

  • **OpenRouter is now available as a BYOK provider.** Bring your own OpenRouter API key to access hundreds of models from a single provider.
  • **Presets sort alphabetically.** The Presets list (formerly "Custom Prompts") now sorts A–Z by default for easier browsing.

Improvements

  • **Japanese and CJK filenames no longer get mangled.** Smart rename no longer inserts separators between every character in CJK output.
  • **Expired BYOK trials stay usable.** An expired trial no longer hard-blocks the app — you can still browse files and explore before upgrading.
  • **Faster, more reliable startup.** The processing queue now waits for licensing to settle before draining, eliminating false "not licensed" errors on launch.
  • **Template test-and-apply is more reliable.** Applying a naming template from the preview now respects rename filters and handles security scopes correctly.

Bug Fixes

  • **Keychain lock no longer stalls processing.** If your Mac's Keychain is locked, NameQuick now shows a clear message and defers work instead of silently failing.
  • **Legacy Keychain entries cleaned up on key removal.** Deleting an API key now removes older duplicate entries that could cause ghost authentications.
  • **Drag-and-drop recognizes existing files again.** Dropping a file that's already in the library no longer creates a duplicate.
  • **Ollama base URL saved correctly.** Custom Ollama endpoints are no longer silently reset.
  • **HUD notifications no longer loop.** Fixed a re-entrancy issue that could cause the notification banner to flash repeatedly.
  • **License status in About window is accurate.** The About panel now reads from the canonical license source instead of a stale cache.
  • **Checkout identity preserved across sessions.** Your customer identity no longer resets when switching between subscription and BYOK flows.
  • **Trial status pill loads on resume.** Reopening the app no longer shows a stale credit count.

Release date: 2026-01-30

Bug Fixes

  • **BYOK trial email login works reliably.** We now recognize trial entitlements even without an active subscription, so your BYOK trial email won’t get rejected.

Release date: 2026-01-28

Bug Fixes

  • **BYOK license no longer lost after Keychain hiccups.** Temporary Keychain access issues no longer clear your saved API keys.
  • **Offline startup works with newer subscription plans.** No longer requires an internet connection on every launch.
  • **Onboarding shows accurate key status.** Setup flow correctly reflects when keys are actually usable.
  • **File list updates more reliably.** Changes to tags and metadata now refresh immediately.
  • **Fixed additional memory leaks.**

Release date: 2026-01-27

New

  • **Per-field required/optional settings.** Each extraction field now has its own required/optional dropdown, replacing the global toggle. This gives you precise control over which fields must have values for a rename to proceed.
  • **In-app update notifications.** A banner now appears when an update is available, with quick access to install or view the changelog.

Improvements

  • **Redesigned AI provider cards.** Provider cards now use accordion behavior—expanding one collapses others. Selection is integrated directly into each card, and the app auto-selects a valid provider when you save a new API key.
  • **More reliable watch folder processing.** Files added during brief monitoring pauses are now caught by a follow-up scan.

Bug Fixes

  • **First file no longer blocked on startup.** Fixed a race condition where the first file detected after launch could be permanently blocked while license validation completed.
  • **Fixed memory leaks during image loading.** Resolved memory growth when viewing many images.
  • **Fixed memory leak in file monitoring.** Addressed a leak that could occur during rename operations.
  • **Local provider status updates correctly.** The connection indicator now reflects actual status changes in real time.
  • **Template field handling is more reliable.** Fixed issues with required field detection, field deletion cleanup, and separator handling.

Release date: 2026-01-24

New

  • **German language support.** The app is now fully localized in German. Language follows your system preference. Feedback on the translations is welcome—reach out via email.

Bug Fixes

  • **Credit tracking is more reliable across app restarts.** Fixed an issue where credit usage could be lost or reset unexpectedly when the app restarted during a billing period. Your usage now persists correctly even if sync timing varies.
  • **Monthly credit rollover timing is more accurate.** Improved scheduling so credit allowances reset at the correct time when a new billing period begins.

Release date: 2026-01-22

Improvements

  • **Local text-only models now work with text PDFs.** Ollama and LM Studio text-only models can process PDFs that contain extractable text. Clear error messages appear when a vision model is needed for images or scanned PDFs.
  • **Quick access to support email.** Help menu and Settings now include direct links to reach support.

Bug Fixes

  • **SVG files process correctly for subscription users.** Fixed an issue where SVG, AI, and EPS files failed when processed through the subscription path.
  • **Rules action editors stay visible.** Tag and comment action editors no longer disappear unexpectedly. Archive folder picker now correctly shows the base folder with relative path.

Release date: 2026-01-21

Bug Fixes

  • **Subscription credits now display correctly.** Fixed an issue where credits showed as 0 for subscription customers despite having available balance.
  • **Shift-click selection works reliably.** File table selection range now behaves correctly when shift-clicking.

Improvements

  • **Fixed memory leaks during inference.** Resolved memory growth when processing many files.
  • **More reliable watch folder processing.**
  • **Better handling of large PDFs.** When a PDF exceeds AI provider limits, NameQuick automatically falls back to text extraction.
  • **Faster background processing.** Auto-processing skips unnecessary UI updates for smoother performance.

Release date: 2026-01-17

Fixes

  • **Subscription credits now display correctly.** Fixed an issue where some subscription customers saw 0 credits available despite having an active plan.

Release date: 2026-01-14

What's New

  • **Local model downloads in Settings** – Local Providers now list recommended Ollama and LM Studio models with hardware guidance and quick download or copy actions.

Improvements

  • **More reliable local model output** – Local prompts now enforce template rules so assembled filenames match transformed fields, and JSON/fenced responses are cleaned automatically.
  • **Cleaner freeform naming on local providers** – Ollama and LM Studio freeform authoring now uses chat endpoints for more consistent responses.
  • **Suffixes now stick with dot separators.** Smart Rename no longer drops suffix text when you use `.` as the separator.
  • **Smart Rename affixes are now global-only.** Any legacy per-folder prefix/suffix overrides are cleared on upgrade.

Release date: 2025-12-24

Completely Redesigned Settings

Settings has been rebuilt from the ground up with native macOS styling. The new design features a clean sidebar with six organized categories—General, Appearance, Notifications, AI, Processing, and About—making it faster to find what you need.

**What's new:**

  • **Instant Search** – Press ⌘F to search across all settings. Select a result to jump directly to that setting, highlighted and ready to adjust.
  • **Keyboard Navigation** – Use ⌘1 through ⌘6 to jump to any category instantly.
  • **Refreshed AI Provider Cards** – Each provider displays its official logo with clear connection status indicators.

Also in This Update

  • **More List Columns** – Six new column options: Last Viewed, Created Date, Modified Date, File Size, Tags, and Comments. Choose up to 3 to display.
  • **API Key Validation Now Tests Real Connectivity** – When you enter an API key for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or Ollama, NameQuick now verifies it actually works by making a test connection. You'll see clear status indicators showing whether your key is valid, invalid, or if the service is temporarily unavailable.

Release date: 2025-12-18

What's New

  • **Try NameQuick Free with Your Own API Key** – New users can now try NameQuick free for 14 days using their own OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or Ollama API key. The trial includes a set number of AI-powered renames to help you evaluate the app before committing.
  • **Subscription Trials for New Customers** – If you prefer not to use your own API key, you can now start a free trial of our managed AI service. Existing customers returning after a break will see a welcome-back flow that recognizes their account.

Improvements

  • **Faster, More Memory-Efficient Image Processing** – Renamed files containing images now process more efficiently with lower memory usage. Large batches of screenshots and photos should no longer cause slowdowns or high memory consumption.
  • **More Reliable Gemini Integration** – Files that Gemini cannot process (due to content restrictions) now display a clear error message instead of retrying silently. Credits are only charged when a rename actually succeeds.

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where upgrading from a trial to a subscription didn't immediately reflect in the app, showing stale "trial" indicators until restart.
  • Fixed an issue where purchased BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) licenses were incorrectly shown trial-related UI elements.
  • Trial periods now use server-verified time to ensure accurate expiration regardless of system clock settings.
  • Fixed unnecessary complexity in AI responses by only including the internal rule log field when templates actually use transformation rules.
  • Fixed a template bug where previously working templates broke. These should be stable now.

Release date: 2025-12-13

Fixes

- Fixed an issue where template-based renaming could produce generic filenames like `_.pdf` instead of extracting meaningful names from your documents. Templates now reliably capture the fields you've defined.

Release date: 2025-12-09

Fixes

- Fixed a rare issue where subscription credits could stay “stuck” on a device after your billing period renewed or your plan changed.

- Fixed an issue where custom prompts that analyze screenshots or images would sometimes ignore the visual content and only consider text. These prompts now correctly take the image itself into account when generating results.

Release date: 2025-12-08

  • Added sorting options to the toolbar. You can now sort files by name, date, size, or type in ascending or descending order.
  • Fixed an issue where custom separators in naming patterns (like dashes, underscores, or spaces) could be lost when reopening or duplicating a pattern. Your patterns now preserve exactly the separators you chose.

Release date: 2025-12-07

  • Fixed custom separators with spaces (like " - ") being stripped down to just the character (e.g., "-"). Filenames now correctly preserve space-surrounded separators for formats like "2024-01-15 - Invoice.pdf".
  • Fixed filename patterns dropping custom literal text. Any static words you add (e.g., "Test") now stay in the assembled filename alongside your extracted fields.
  • Fixed template testing failures caused by overly strict validation of AI response audit logs. Processing now correctly succeeds when all required fields are extracted.

Release date: 2025-12-07

  • Fixed issue where duplicate files could incorrectly receive the same AI-generated name as a previously processed file.
  • Fixed template preview incorrectly showing "Could not extract" errors when fields were actually extracted successfully.
  • Fixed sidebar disappearing after closing Settings.
  • Added tooltips to sidebar tabs explaining their purpose (All Files, To Process, Processed).
  • Fixed files skipped by rename filters staying stuck in "To Process" instead of moving to "Processed".
  • Added "Remove from Library" option to remove files from the app without deleting them from disk.
  • Added "Clear All" button in the Processed tab toolbar to quickly remove all processed items from the library.

Release date: 2025-12-04

  • Improved reliability for managed AI processing.
  • Added recovery handling for corrupted template files with a clear warning when detected.
  • Fixed issue where file renames could appear successful without actually renaming the file.
  • Fixed processing delay setting not saving between sessions.

Release date: 2025-12-04

  • Fixed an issue where auto-processing with Custom Prompts would ignore document content, resulting in poor rename quality.

Release date: 2025-12-03

  • Fixed a bug where Custom Prompts created in earlier versions weren't visible after updating to v2.10.00 or v2.10.01. Your prompts are automatically restored on launch.

Release date: 2025-12-03

  • Fixed an issue where some users were shown the onboarding flow again after restarting the app, even though they had already completed it.

NameQuick 2.1.0

A fresh start. This release reimagines how you get started with NameQuick and makes the whole experience feel more polished and intuitive.

New Onboarding Experience

Getting set up is now smoother than ever. A guided flow walks you through choosing how you want to rename files—whether that's AI-powered Smart Rename, structured Templates, or freeform Custom Prompts.

Presets

Templates and Custom Prompts are now unified under Presets, with major upgrades to both:

**Templates** got a complete overhaul:

  • **Visual pattern builder** with drag-and-drop chips that show exactly how your filename will look
  • **Per-field separators** so you can mix dashes, underscores, and spaces however you need
  • **Live preview** updates as you build, so you always know what you're getting
  • **Test your templates** while building and iterate until they're perfect

**Custom Prompts** now have a cleaner, more prominent UI that makes them easier to create and manage.

Smarter Rules

The rules engine is now easier to use and more powerful:

  • **Card-based layout** makes it simple to see and manage your rules at a glance
  • **Archive and Trash actions** give you more options beyond just moving files
  • **Logic operators** let you combine conditions for precise control (AND/OR)
  • **Date conditions** for rules that trigger based on when files were created or modified

Refreshed Design

The whole app feels cleaner—modern glass-style buttons, smooth sidebar transitions, and polished drop zones throughout.

And much more!!

Release date: 2025-11-29

  • Another small bug fix for subscription users.

# NameQuick 2.0.46

Release date: 2025-11-29

  • Small bug fix for subscription users.

# NameQuick 2.0.45

  • Fixed a license logging crash when disk space is low by serializing writes on an actor and gating the HUD to foreground state.

# NameQuick 2.0.44 — Credit balance refresh & improved PDF handling

Release date: 2025-11-26

What's fixed

  • Credits now properly refresh when your subscription billing cycle renews. Previously, some users saw their old credit balance until they logged out and back in.
  • Cached credit data is now automatically invalidated when a new billing period begins, ensuring you always see your current balance.
  • Large PDFs now process more reliably. Increased internal compression target from 15 MB to 45 MB, allowing high-resolution scanned documents to succeed where they previously failed.
  • Oversized PDFs now show a clear error message instead of failing silently. If a PDF exceeds the 50 MB processing limit, you'll see exactly what the problem is and how to fix it.

NameQuick 2.0.43 — Smoother BYOK licensing

Release date: 2025-11-17

What’s fixed - Removes the “license validation” warning HUD that could briefly appear on launch for customers using BYOK/Local license keys, even when the license was fully valid.

NameQuick 2.0.42 — Reliable renames on macOS 26.1

Release date: 2025-11-10

What’s fixed - Fixed a rare issue where some files couldn’t be opened or renamed after using NameQuick on macOS 26.1. - Files renamed with NameQuick now behave normally in Finder and other apps.

Other improvements - Clearer messages if a rename can’t safely complete. - Credit counter now refreshes correctly after a renewal.

Compatibility & notes - Recommended for all users on macOS 26.1. - No changes to your data or settings.
• Small fixes and improvements
• Fix: resolves a rare freeze on macOS Sonoma where the sidebar repeatedly hid and showed, spiking CPU and stalling the app. NameQuick now opens and stays responsive.

• Smarter duplicate-name handling: when a target filename already exists, NameQuick now automatically appends a counter (e.g., ” (2)“) and completes the rename, instead of showing a”file already exists” error. This keeps your flow uninterrupted and preserves the original extension.

  • Gemini model updates (current + compatible): the selector now shows the latest 2.5 and 2.0 language models that work with NameQuick’s generateContent flow, and removes legacy/unsupported entries.

    • Added: Gemini 2.5 Pro, 2.5 Flash, 2.5 Flash‑Lite, 2.5 Flash‑Lite (Preview 09‑2025); Gemini 2.0 Flash, 2.0 Flash‑Lite, 2.0 Flash (Experimental).
    • Removed: Gemini 1.5/1.0 families, Live‑only, and TTS models (not compatible with this flow).
    • Automatic migration: if you previously selected a removed model (e.g., 1.5/1.0, Live preview, old Flash‑Lite preview ID), you’ll switch to the closest 2.5 equivalent on launch and in the selector.

  • Friendlier error messages: if a template can’t be satisfied (e.g., missing required fields), the message explains what to change (relax rules, add examples, or try Smart Rename) instead of a generic contract violation.

Notes - “Free/Paid” in the Gemini selector means the model offers a free tier but may bill beyond free quota depending on your Google account usage. - If you had a 1.5/1.0 model selected, you’ll be moved to a 2.5 equivalent automatically.

• Implemented a bug fix focusing on size limitations experienced by a user
  • Some crash fixes

NameQuick 2.0.37

  • Large PDF uploads are now processed reliably without memory spikes. We preprocess PDFs before reading them, keeping memory use low and preventing crashes on very large files.
  • Fixed vertical overflow issues in the Settings view for improved layout and accessibility
  • General stability improvements and crash fixes

NameQuick 2.0.36

  • Drag to reorder folders in the sidebar
  • Exclude Subfolders now sticks reliably
  • Re-adding removed folders works immediately (no extra permission steps)
  • Better filename fidelity and control
    • Parentheses and square brackets are now preserved in names (e.g., “(Company)”, “[25 AUG ’25]”).
    • Default cleanup only removes macOS‑illegal characters; your custom prompt’s formatting is kept intact.
    • “Clean filenames” remains optional for stricter ASCII/transliteration, if you need it.
  • General stability improvements

NameQuick 2.0.35 — Precise Folder Scope & Subfolders

Highlights

  • Per‑folder subfolder control: include or exclude subfolders for each watch folder from the sidebar context menu.

How to Use

  • Right‑click a watch folder in the sidebar → toggle “Include Subfolders”.

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where custom prompts did not run when dragging and dropping a file into the app in subscription mode.

NameQuick 2.0.34

Features

  • Added a global Clean filenames toggle under Preferences → General & Permissions → Defaults. When enabled, NameQuick transliterates German umlauts (ä→ae, ö→oe, ü→ue, Ä→Ae, Ö→Oe, Ü→Ue, ß→ss) and keeps only letters, numbers, single spaces, underscores, and dashes across Smart Rename, Templates, and Custom Prompt flows.

Improvements

  • Updated the rename preview and documentation to call out the exact cleanup rules so teams know what stays and what goes before uploading files.

Fixes

  • Replaced the credit usage queue with a GRDB-backed worker that keeps retries off the main thread, prevents runaway JSON re-encoding, and eliminates the high-CPU watchdog terminations some users saw when Polar was slow.

Cleanup

  • Removed the deprecated Smart Rename and Basic settings tabs now that the Craft-style sidebar covers those options.

NameQuick 2.0.32

Fixes

Template Consistency

  • Drag & Drop and Global Shortcut: Both operations now consistently apply prefixes and suffixes. ### UI Layout Improvements
  • Settings Window Responsiveness: Fixed layout overflow issues at compact window widths.

v2.0.31 — BYOK Stability & Prompt Manager Refresh

Improvements

  • Claude BYOK pacing – Batch rename jobs automatically throttle requests so you can push more files without tripping Anthropic’s burst limits or seeing 429 errors.
  • Managed prompt creation – Fixed a crash that occurred when Managed (Plug & Play) subscribers pressed “New Prompt.” The add/edit form now opens inline instead of stacking a second sheet, so the NSWindowAlreadyHasSheetException path is gone.
  • Watch folder stability – Eliminated duplicate watch-folder entries that caused _MergeError crashes at launch, especially on macOS Sequoia. Saved profiles are cleaned automatically and add/remove now handle path variants safely.

Bug Fixes

  • License activation no longer crashes. If something goes wrong while activating, you’ll just see the warning message.
  • Connectivity validation keeps running. Skipping a recent success no longer refreshes the cache, so periodic checks still execute and catch environment/token issues.

Performance

  • Credit balance sync now surfaces instantly on launch. Cached snapshots load immediately while Polar refresh runs in the background, eliminating the multi-second stall when reopening the app.
  • Reduced Polar auth chatter. Successful connectivity checks are cached for 10 minutes so relaunches skip the redundant HEAD request that previously blocked credit syncing.
  • Faster customer resolution retries. Background refreshes reuse cached IDs and only fan out to Polar when metadata changes, trimming cold-start latency.

What’s Improved

  • Custom prompts moved to Processing settings. Find “Manage Custom Prompts…” inside Settings → Processing ### Bug Fixes
  • Crash on launch eliminated. The Accessibility relaunch prompt now always runs on the main actor, so fresh installs and permission changes no longer trip a Swift precondition and terminate the app.

What’s Improved

  • Quick actions stay visible even before you select files. The floating process bar no longer disappears when you load a batch, making it obvious how to kick off renames and when actions are disabled versus ready to run.

What’s Fixed

  • Never fall back to BYOK for managed accounts. If we’re momentarily offline from Polar, NameQuick now keeps your Managed AI license active, works from cached access, and automatically returns to the managed Gemini service once reconnected.
  • Clear warning and logging when service is offline. You’ll see a brief “Managed AI is temporarily offline…” banner if we’re working from cached access, and the diagnostics log captures the fallback event so support can diagnose it quickly.

Improvements

  • Adaptive menu bar icon – The menu bar symbol now respects light and dark appearances, keeping contrast consistent even when the system theme switches mid-session.
  • Quickly hide the menu bar icon – Added a Show Menu Bar Icon toggle in Appearance and General settings so you can run NameQuick without the status-item when you prefer a cleaner menu bar.

Fixes

  • Correct vision model command – Updated the “Add Vision Model” action in Settings so it now copies ollama run qwen2.5vl:32b, ensuring the suggested Ollama command works without manual correction.

Notes

  • No other changes in this build; this release focuses on menu bar polish and one Ollama setup fix.

v2.0.24

## Features

## Fixes

v2.0.23 — Queue Management Made Smoother

### What’s New

  • New queue management popover – Introducing a dedicated queue popover where you can view all your queued rename files. Remove individual items or clear the entire queue with a single click—everything updates instantly with live badge counts.

Improvements & Fixes

  • Editing made stable – Fixed an issue where the app would crash when trying to edit your default naming template. You can now safely customize all your templates without any hiccups.
  • License management simplified – Resolved a quirk where the app would sometimes re-activate itself after you’d reset your license. Once you unlink your license, it stays unlinked until you choose to activate it again.

v2.0.22 — Fresh UI for macOS Tahoe

What’s New

  • Redesigned for macOS Tahoe – Beautiful new user interface optimized for Apple’s latest macOS, with modern visual refinements throughout the app.
  • Vector artists rejoice – Drop in AI, SVG, or EPS files and NameQuick will import, preview, and rename them just like your photos.
  • Templates that feel native – The Templates screen now looks and behaves like Finder. Quick actions sit right where you expect them, and the pencil button jumps straight into editing.

Bug Fixes & Improvements

  • Memory leak fixed – Eliminated a critical memory leak that could cause crashes during extended use—the app now runs reliably for hours without issues.
  • Remembers your style – Fixed persistence issues—separators, prompts, and other preferences now properly stick between sessions.
  • Brackets now allowed – Fixed filename sanitization—you can now use bracket characters in generated filenames without them being stripped out.
  • Custom prompts persist – Fixed custom prompt overrides not being saved—your selected prompts are now properly restored when you restart the app.
  • Clearer UI controls – Added visible Done button and Cancel/Save buttons to the custom prompt manager—no more relying on the Escape key to close dialogs.
  • Visible dialog buttons – Fixed invisible button issue—all confirmation dialogs now show clear Cancel and action buttons (like the cache-clear dialog).
  • Instant template edits – Fixed template editing behavior—tap the pencil to open the editor, and rename from the context menu when you really mean it.
  • Automatic upgrades – Fixed watch folder compatibility—existing watch folders pick up vector support automatically without extra setup.
  • Stability improvements – Squashed numerous bugs affecting app stability and performance.
  • UI polish – Fixed various interface glitches and visual inconsistencies throughout the app.

Why You’ll Love It

  • Broader format support means fewer workflow detours.
  • Smarter AI helps you finish renaming faster.
  • A more native interface keeps everything familiar and simple.
  • Built‑in safeguards protect your large creative files without any extra effort.

v2.0.21 – Move files based on rules

Features

New Rules Engine - Powerful File Organization Automation

NameQuick now includes a comprehensive rules engine that gives you fine-grained control over how files are processed and organized. Create custom rules to automatically move files, set metadata, and control processing based on rich file analysis—all before or after AI processing.

Two-Phase Processing System

  • Before Rename: Rules evaluate files as they enter watch folders, perfect for initial routing and organization
  • After Rename: Rules can run again after AI completes renaming, allowing you to organize files based on their final names

Powerful Actions

Rules can perform multiple actions automatically:

File Organization: - Move files using smart path templates with dynamic placeholders - Example: Move iPhone photos to Photos/{camera_make}/{date_taken}/

Finder Metadata: - Set color labels, Finder comments, and tags - Example: Tag invoices with “Finance” and set red color label

Date Management: - Set custom creation/modification dates - Useful for organizing scanned documents by actual document date

Template Routing (folder‑based): - Configure a watch folder to always use a specific AI template - Note: Rules‑based “auto‑choose template” is not enabled in this release

Smart Path Templates

Create dynamic destination paths using powerful placeholders:

  • {original} - Original filename without extension
  • {date_taken} - EXIF date taken or creation date
  • {camera_make} / {camera_model} - Camera information
  • {year}, {month}, {day} - Current date components
  • {parent} - Source folder name
  • {width} / {height} - Image/video dimensions

Example: Photos/{camera_make}/{year}/{month}-{day}/{original}.{extension}

Notes and safety: - Moves require selecting a base destination folder (security‑scoped bookmark) - Only relative subpaths are allowed; .. and absolute paths are rejected - Collisions are handled automatically; use {counter} to force numbering - Trailing / keeps the original filename (e.g., {date_taken}/ → appends source name)

Real-World Use Cases

Photo Organization:

If: Camera make contains "iPhone" AND has GPS data
Then: Move to "Photos/iPhone/{date_taken}/" AND tag "Personal"

Document Organization:

If: Filename contains "invoice" AND extension is "pdf"
Then: Move to "Finance/Invoices/{year}/{month}/" AND set red color label

Screenshot Management:

If: Filename contains "Screenshot" AND width > 1920
Then: Move to "Screenshots/High-Res/{date}/" AND tag "Design"

Enhanced Rules Editor

The Rules tab in Settings now provides: - Intuitive predicate builder with smart suggestions - Phase filtering to manage before/after processing rules separately - Quick rule summaries showing conditions and actions - Schema-aware import/export for sharing rule configurations - Real-time validation and error checking - New “Subpath Template” field with inline validation - Clickable help popovers for Move to, Subpath Template, Finder tags/comment/color, and File dates

This new rules engine transforms NameQuick from a simple file processor into a sophisticated document management system that can handle complex organizational workflows automatically.

Fixes

  • Added regression coverage around rules import/export to catch malformed content or media predicates before they ship.
  • Resolved BYOK OpenAI audio failures by mapping the new GPT-4o transcription models, so .wav/.mp3 jobs no longer 404 when calling /v1/audio/transcriptions, and now run a two-step flow (transcribe → rename) to feed the transcript into your selected model.
  • Restored Smart Rename’s custom prompt handoff for drag/drop, menu commands, and watch folders after the batch pipeline migration.

v2.0.20

## Features

## Fixes

v2.0.19 — Create Your Own Prompts

Your naming rules, automated.

You can now write custom prompts for how you want files renamed. Pick one from the toolbar, and NameQuick will use it for every file until you change it. They also work with watch folders for automatic renaming.

Creating a prompt Settings → AI → Manage Custom Prompts. Write your instructions like “date first, then project name” and save. It’ll show up in your toolbar dropdown.

Using prompts The toolbar dropdown now has three sections: Smart Rename, Templates, and Custom Prompts. Pick a custom prompt and the Play button uses it. Switch back to Smart Rename anytime.

Auto-rename with watch folders Click on the Can u → Prompt → pick your custom prompt. New files dropped there automatically rename using that prompt. The folder chip shows which prompt is active.

How it works: - Play button: Uses the prompt shown in the toolbar - Watch folders: Use their own assigned prompts (independent of toolbar) - Templates: Override prompts when selected

Simple. Your file naming system, running on autopilot.

Fixes - Large media drag & drop: Provider‑aware limits—Gemini supports up to 500 MB (with a quick confirm at 100–500 MB); OpenAI audio above 25 MB is blocked with a helpful tip. - Templates: Fixed {original} not resolving. File-based placeholders now work correctly in template renames ({original}, {extension}, {parent}, {size}, {dimensions}).

Known bugs - Drag & drop not processing the same file.

v2.0.18 — New Onboarding & Cleaner Mac Menu Bar

This release introduces a brand‑new onboarding experience, some UI polish, and undo action.

What’s New

  • All‑new onboarding flow with just‑in‑time prompts and a faster path to your first rename.
  • Undo renames from grid view, list view, and the Mac menu bar.

Improvements

  • Cleaner Mac menu bar UI.
  • The processing spinner is now in the Mac menu bar.
  • Reduced icon color variety for less visual clutter.

NameQuick v2.0.17 (Hotfix)

Fixes a login issue affecting subscription customers using email aliases with a “+” (plus) sign.

Highlights

  • Email login: Addresses like name+tag@domain.com now work as expected.

Thanks to the report that helped me pinpoint this quickly.

— Josef

Quick hotfix to eliminate unexpected macOS Keychain password prompts when opening the AI settings.

Highlights

  • No more login Keychain prompts when viewing AI settings.
  • Smoother settings experience with non‑interactive Keychain reads.

Thanks for the fast reports — this should make settings feel calm again.

— Josef

NameQuick v2.0.15

This release makes Templates rock‑solid with Claude, fixes an OpenAI schema edge case, and keeps spreadsheets fast and stable.

Highlights

  • Templates (Claude): Reliable structured output with consistent results.
  • OpenAI schema: Fixes rare “type: None” error in structured output.
  • Spreadsheets: Stable memory + faster previews for large XLSX files.
  • Error messages: Clearer, provider‑specific guidance.

Improvements

  • Templates (Claude)
    • Uses Claude Tools with an input schema to return clean JSON (no chatty responses).
    • Normalizes template field keys under the hood for tool compatibility; your template labels are unchanged.
  • Spreadsheet handling
    • Size‑aware parsing and smarter sampling keep memory bounded on large workbooks.
    • Faster, steadier previews for DOCX/PPTX/XLSX.
  • Error messages
    • Clearer document guidance and provider‑specific mapping (no generic attributions).
    • Unified wording for unsupported actions; suggests the next best path.

Fixes

  • Templates (Claude): Resolves “response_format: Extra inputs are not permitted” by switching to Tools.
  • OpenAI: Fixes rare structured‑output error (“schema must be type: object, got None”).
  • Office previews: Prevents spikes from embedded thumbnails; steadier memory.
  • Spreadsheet parsing: Resolves a leak in XLSX handling and aborts cleanly on extraction failures with clear guidance.

Thanks for using NameQuick and for all the feedback — it helps a ton.

— Josef

NameQuick v2.0.14

Release notes

NameQuick v2.0.14

This one focuses on smooth, reliable background processing and better document support — plus a new option for BYOK folks: Claude (Anthropic).

Highlights

  • New: You can now rename Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files (DOCX, PPTX, XLSX).
  • BYOK: Claude (Anthropic) joins the lineup with native PDF + image analysis and explicit model selection.

New

  • Word (.docx): Smart, clean names for your documents — fast previews included.
  • PowerPoint (.pptx): Uses slide text to suggest clear context-aware names.
  • Excel (.xlsx, .xlsm): Concise, informative names for your spreadsheets.
  • Quiet background mode: Keep NameQuick tucked away — it keeps watching your folders and renaming files.
  • BYOK: Added Claude (Anthropic) as a provider — native PDF support via Messages API, image analysis, explicit model selection, key validation, and per‑item cost shown in recent history.

Improvements

  • Folder recovery: If your Mac sleeps or a drive disconnects, NameQuick resumes and catches up automatically.
  • Startup experience: Files already in your watch folders appear instantly. Only new or changed files auto‑process (based on your settings).
  • UI: Cleaner, calmer loading indicator in the grid.

Fixes

  • Automatic processing could pause for some folders.
  • Files added later to a watched folder now process as expected.
  • PPTX files were sometimes flagged as unsupported — they now work consistently.

Thanks for using NameQuick and for all the feedback — it helps a ton.

— Josef
